items.headline: Major commercial project breaks ground on West San Carlos
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial development is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, representing significant investment in San José's commercial corridor. This is the only major non-residential project in this permit batch and could bring new business activity or jobs to the area.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is offering a business license amnesty program targeted at shopping centers, likely to bring unlicensed or non-compliant businesses into legal standing. This could affect how local retail operates and what taxes/fees the city collects from the business community.

items.headline: City unveils economic incentives for retail and small business growth
items.summary: Cupertino is introducing new incentive programs to attract and support retail, small businesses, and startups. Combined with outreach efforts and a "Shop Local" campaign, this signals city efforts to strengthen the local commercial district and downtown economy.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up becomes annual city event
items.summary: San José officially designated the Great American Litter Pick-up as an annual city-sponsored special event. The city authorized spending and accepting donations for this environmental cleanup initiative.

items.headline: Planning Commission to Study San Antonio Road Area Development
items.summary: The commission will examine land use and transportation alternatives for the San Antonio Road area, potentially affecting how this corridor develops. This study could shape future zoning, traffic patterns, and new projects in that part of Palo Alto.

items.headline: City Updates Retail Rules to Boost Shopping District Vitality
items.summary: Palo Alto is amending zoning and building codes to implement retail policies from its Comprehensive Plan, aimed at supporting local shopping areas. The changes could affect what types of retail businesses can operate where and how storefronts are designed.

items.headline: City approves power grid easement across wastewater facility
items.summary: San José approved an easement agreement allowing LS Power Grid California to install electrical transmission infrastructure across the San José-Santa Clara Regional Wastewater Facility. This infrastructure project will affect a major city facility and may have implications for future utility improvements in the region.

items.headline: San José, Santa Clara pursue direct potable reuse water facility
items.summary: The city entered exclusive negotiations with Santa Clara and Valley Water to acquire land for a future Direct Potable Reuse facility. This project would treat wastewater for drinking water—a significant drought adaptation strategy that will affect the region's water supply.
